revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Hitsujisaki-jinja (Mano, Ishinomaki)]][[ja:零羊崎神社 (石巻市真野)]] {{Expand Japanese|零羊崎神社 (石巻市真野)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hitsujisaki Shrine (Q11660145) | native_name = 零羊崎神社 | image = Hitsujisaki jinja (Mano) Haiden.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hitsujisaki Shrine (Q11660145) | coordinates = {{coord|38.47247222222222|141.37775}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Toyotama-hime|qid=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|de|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|es|Toyotama-hime|fa|تویوتاما-هیمه|fi|Otohime|fr|Otohime|id|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ|ko|도요타마히메|mni|ꯇꯣꯌꯣꯇꯃ-ꯍꯤꯃꯦ|pl|Toyotama-hime|ru|Тоётама-химэ|simple|Toyotama-hime|sv|Otohime|uk|Тойотама-хіме|vi|Toyotama-hime|zh|豐玉毘賣命|qid=Q2329261|1=Toyotama-hime}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hitsujisaki Shrine'''|零羊崎神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Myojin Taisha|lt=Myōjin Taisha|en|Myojin Taisha|fr|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|simple|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11658590|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 43 ** P155: {{ill|Q11616108|ltq=Q11616108|qid=Q11616108|1=Kusano Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Katoriitsuno- Shrine|lt=Katoriitsuno- Shrine|qid=Q135040002|1=Katoriitsuno- Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q270814|ltq=Q270814|qid=Q270814|1=Ishinomaki}} * {{ill|Q907389|ltq=Q907389|qid=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}} * {{ill|Oshika District, Miyagi|lt=Oshika district|ceb|Oshika Gun|en|Oshika District, Miyagi|fr|District d'Oshika|id|Distrik Oshika, Miyagi|it|Distretto di Oshika|ja|牡鹿郡|ko|오시카군|pl|Powiat Oshika|ru|Осика (уезд)|sr|Област Ошика|uk|Повіт Осіка|zh_yue|牡鹿郡|zh|牡鹿郡|qid=Q1131921|1=Oshika District, Miyagi}} == coordinate location (P625) == https://web.archive.org/web/20151121015607/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/271603.html == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182080 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917284|ltq=Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank|ja|正五位|ko|정5위|zh|正五位|qid=Q11123280|1=Senior Fifth Rank}} * {{ill|Q11071127|ltq=Q11071127|qid=Q11071127|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} == architectural style (P149) == * Hitsujisaki-jinja (Mano, Ishinomaki) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Hitsushisakino Shrine|lt=Hitsushisakino Shrine|qid=Q135040001|1=Hitsushisakino Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '宮城県石巻市真野字内山18', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '宮城県石巻市湊', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Hitsujisaki-jinja (Mano, Ishinomaki)}} [[JA:零羊崎神社 (石巻市真野)]] {{wikidata link|Q11660145}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Reiyōsaki Shrine |Image = [[File:Hitsujisaki jinja (Mano) Haiden.JPG|270px|Reiyōsaki Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden |Location = {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|qid=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ishinomaki City|Q270814|qid=Q270814|1=Ishinomaki}} Mano, Uchiyama 18 |Coordinates = {{Coord|38|28|20.86|N|141|22|39.90|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Reiyōsaki Shrine}} |Deity = {{ill|qid=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}
Akitsuhiko no Mikoto
{{ill|lt=Hayakitsuhiko-Hayakitsuhime|Q11326733|qid=Q11326733|lt=Hayakitsuhime no Mikoto|1=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime}} |Rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Grand Shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}}) {{ill|lt=Ronsya|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Honden = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other name = Shiratori-sama |Pilgrimage site etc. = |Annual festival = 9th day of the 9th month (lunar calendar) |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Reiyōsaki Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Hitsujisaki jinja (Mano) Gate.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Torii]] [[File:Hitsujisaki jinja (Mano) Honden.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Honden]] '''Reiyōsaki Shrine''' (れいようさきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mano, {{ill|lt=Ishinomaki City|Q270814|qid=Q270814|1=Ishinomaki}}, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|qid=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}) {{ill|lt=Ronsya|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following three (according to the stone pillar at the approach): * '''{{ill|qid=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}''' * '''Akitsuhiko no Mikoto''' * '''{{ill|lt=Hayakitsuhiko-Hayakitsuhime|Q11326733|qid=Q11326733|lt=Hayakitsuhime no Mikoto|1=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime}}''' However, some sources, such as *Heisei Junpaiki Engishiki Inland Mutsu One Hundred Shrines*, introduce Toyotama-hime no Mikoto and {{ill|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} as the enshrined deities. === History === === Founding === The date of founding is unknown. *Fūnai Fudoki, Volume 13* ({{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|qid=Q7690|1=1772}}, {{ill|lt=An’ei era|Q1193910|qid=Q1193910|1=An'ei}} 1)Included in *Fūnai Fudoki, Vol. 2* (1975). states that it is unclear when the shrine was enshrined. === Debate over identification as a Myōjin Taisha === During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, this shrine came to be identified with the Myōjin Taisha “Reiyōsaki Shrine.” “Reiyōsaki Shrine” appears in the *{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}* under the entry for the 27th day of the 1st month of {{ill|lt=Jōgan era (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}), as one of the 267 shrines granted promotion in {{ill|lt=Shinto ranks|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, having been elevated to {{ill|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|Q11071127|qid=Q11071127|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}In the entry for the 27th day of the 1st month of Jōgan 1 (859), the *Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku* reads: “Shōgoi no ge, kun yon-tō Shiha-hiko no kami. Kun go-tō Haibei Shi no kami. Kun roku-tō Reiyōsaki no kami. Jugo-i no jō, kun yon-tō Shiha-hime no kami, all Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade.” Thus, it is thought that prior to promotion the rank may have been Shōgoi no ge, kun roku-tō, though it is also possible that it was promoted from an unranked kun roku-tō.. In addition, in the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}* of {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō era|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5), it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, and in *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}*, Volume 3, it is recorded as a shrine subject to Myōjin rituals. However, the name Reiyōsaki Shrine disappears from later historical sources, and disputes arose during the Edo period. In *Ōu Kankisekirōshi* written in {{ill|lt=1719|Q6902|qid=Q6902|1=1719}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō era|Q1145048|qid=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 4)Included in *Sendai Sōsho, Vol. 15: Ōu Kankisekirōshi (Upper)* (1972). and *Fūnai Meisekishi* written in {{ill|lt=1741|Q7573|qid=Q7573|1=1741}} ({{ill|lt=Kanpō era|Q1145798|qid=Q1145798|1=Kanpō}} 1)Included in *Sendai Sōsho, Vol. 8* (1972)., there is no mention of this shrine. However, in *Fūnai Fudoki, Volume 13* written in An’ei 1 (1772), the shrine is mentioned in the entry for Mano Village. According to that account, as noted above, it was not clear when the shrine was enshrined; it was called Shiratori Shrine and was said to be the tutelary shrine of Mano Village, and local people said it was the ancient Reiyōsaki Shrine. As for the reason it was considered Reiyōsaki Shrine, the account states that Reiyōsaki Shrine was originally located at Makiyama in Minato Village, but after a fire it was relocated to Maruyama in Mano Village, where the {{ill|lt=Kasai clan|Q6373969|qid=Q6373969|1=Kasai clan}} had sacred fields. Therefore, markets were held before and after festivals and villagers gathered. As a result, the place where markets were held came to be called Miyakōji, and the place where the sacred fields had been came to be called Miyata; these remains still exist today. Moreover, because the shrine grounds face the direction of {{ill|lt=Sheep|Q20128396|qid=Q20128396|1=wèi}} (the “hitsuji” direction), villagers considered this shrine to be Reiyōsaki Shrine. However, *Fūnai Fudoki, Volume 13* continues by stating that this theory is in fact not correct: at the end of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}}, when the Kasai clan lost its territory and perished, Reiyōsaki Shrine also fell into ruin, and the mikoshi was moved to the Makiyama Kannon Hall in Minato Village. This mikoshi is no longer seen, and the divine name of Reiyōsaki Shrine was changed to Hakusan Shrine. It concludes by stating that these events were due to the machinations of Buddhist monks and are truly lamentable. The entry for Minato Village in *Fūnai Fudoki, Volume 13* also describes the Reiyōsaki Shrine of Minato Village. According to it, the Reiyōsaki Shrine of Minato Village is currently called Hakusan Shrine and enshrines Toyotama-hiko no Mikoto; it was the foremost {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} in Oshika District and was in ancient times a grand shrine; and it serves as the tutelary shrine of the Seven Villages of Minato. Furthermore, in the description of Makiyama Daibikaku (Kannon Hall), it states that the site was the location of the ancient Reiyōsaki Shrine, which Buddhist monks seized, concealing the name Reiyōsaki Shrine and calling it Makiyama Kannon. *Inai Town History* records that there was a dispute between the Reiyōsaki Shrine of Minato Village and this shrine. According to it, when the yamabushi ({{ill|lt=Shugendō practitioners|Q2309838|qid=Q2309838|1=Yamabushi}}) of Fumyōin claimed that the Hakusan Shrine at Makiyama was Reiyōsaki Shrine, Kanshuin of Mano Village strongly protested, and the dispute was brought before the domain authorities. By summons dated the 26th day of the 2nd month of {{ill|lt=1768|Q7675|qid=Q7675|1=1768}} ({{ill|lt=Meiwa era|Q1146161|qid=Q1146161|1=Meiwa}} 5), the monk of Kanshuin appeared before the Sendai Magistrate’s Office on March 3 and was questioned about the matter of Reiyōsaki Shrine and the relationship between Fumyōin, Makiyama Hakusan Shrine, and Mano Reiyōsaki Shrine. Kanshuin responded that because Mano Village had suffered successive years of poor harvests and found it difficult to repair the shrine buildings, the mikoshi that had long been kept was temporarily entrusted to Makiyama Hakusan Shrine, and therefore the mikoshi did not originally belong to Makiyama. The Magistrate’s Office accepted this, ruled that Reiyōsaki Shrine should be worshiped as the tutelary shrine of Mano Village, and thus Kanshuin’s claim was successfully upheldFrom *Inai Town History, Part 9: Religion, Chapter 1: Shrines and Halls*. *Inai Town History* (1960).. === After the Meiji period === After the {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|qid=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} of the {{ill|lt=Meiji period|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine came to be called “Reiyōsaki Shrine.” In {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|qid=Q7761|1=1874}} (Meiji 7), under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was listed as a village shrine and later designated a shrine receiving offerings of ritual food and heihaku. In Meiji 20 ({{ill|lt=1887|Q7826|qid=Q7826|1=1887}}), the shrine buildings were lost to fire, and at the time it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings, it was housed in a temporary shrine. The present shrine buildings were constructed thereafter. === Local information === '''Address''' * Mano, Uchiyama 18, {{ill|lt=Ishinomaki City|Q270814|qid=Q270814|1=Ishinomaki}}, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|qid=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} === Notes === {{Help:Footnotes}} {{reflist}} === References === *{{Cite book|ja|author=Inai Town History Compilation Committee (ed.)|year=1960|title=Inai Town History|publisher=Inai Town Office|isbn= }} *{{Cite book|ja|author=Kuroita Katsumi; Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee (eds.)|year=1966|title=Kokushi Taikei, Vol. 4: {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}|series={{ill|lt=Kokushi Taikei|Q10926780|qid=Q10926780|1=Kokushi Taikei}}|publisher={{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755|qid=Q4346755|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}}|isbn= }} *{{Cite book|ja|author=Suzuki Shōzō (ed.)|year=1972|title=Sendai Sōsho Vol. 8: Fūnai Meisekishi and others|series=Sendai Sōsho|publisher=Hōbundō Shuppan Hanbai|isbn= }} (Reprint by Sendai Sōsho Publication Association; reprint of the 1928 edition) *{{Cite book|ja|author=Suzuki Shōzō (ed.)|year=1972|title=Sendai Sōsho Vol. 15: Ōu Kankisekirōshi (Upper)|series=Sendai Sōsho|publisher=Hōbundō Shuppan Hanbai|isbn= }} (Reprint by Sendai Sōsho Publication Association; reprint of the 1928 edition) *{{Cite book|ja|author=Tanabe Marefumi (ed.); Suzuki Shōzō (rev.)|year=1975|title=Sendai Sōsho: Fūnai Fudoki Vol. 2|series=Sendai Sōsho: Fūnai Fudoki|publisher=Hōbundō Shuppan Hanbai|isbn= }} (Reprint by Sendai Sōsho Publishing Association; reprint of the 1893 edition) *{{Cite book|ja|author=Honda Kanemasa|year=1997|title=Heisei Junpaiki — Lower: Engishiki Inland Mutsu One Hundred Shrines|series=Engishiki Inland Mutsu One Hundred Shrines|publisher=Jinja Shinpōsha|isbn=4-89698-042-5}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Reiyōsaki Shrine (Minato, Ishinomaki)|Q11660144|qid=Q11660144|1=Hitsujisaki Shrine}} – A shrine of the same name located in Ishinomaki City; also considered a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha ronsya|1=UNKNOWN}}. === External links === {{Commons|Category:Hitsujisaki-jinja (Mano, Ishinomaki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/271603.html Reiyōsaki Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation”) {{Shinto navbox}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hitsujisaki Shrine (Mano, Ishinomaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hitsujisaki Shrine (Mano, Ishinomaki)]][[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:石巻市の歴史]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ishinomaki, Miyagi]]
